data.label: xArm Cell Lease
data.description: Acquires ONE `xarm_fleet` runner for the whole sample-handling run and releases it on exit. Everything inside — scene seeding and every job's plan/execute/grasp — runs on the same held runner by CONTAINMENT (the body steps + the pick/place/swap SubWorkflows inherit the held `runner.<id>` namespace), so the persistent move_group planning scene seeded in (A) is the same scene the jobs in (B) plan against. The `requirements` pick WHICH runner to hold (an xArm6); the held lease is parked write-once so the runner stays warm across both Maps. Body steps are plain `executor` — they do NOT claim their own unit (that would deadlock against the held one), they ride the lease.

data.label: Seed Inventory into Scene
data.description: Maps over the bound `run_inventory` asset collection (alias `inv`), scattering one body token per sample. Each body token carries the inventory row as `item` (token-resident itemVar). The body pushes that sample into move_group's persistent planning scene as a box CollisionObject. The gathered per-element value (named by resultVar `seeded`) is the body's `{seeded}` output. Runs under the enclosing cell lease so every add_object hits the SAME held move_group scene.

data.label: Add Sample to Scene
data.description: Map body. ROS `call_service` to `/add_object` (`aithericon_motion_bridge/srv/AddObject`) — pushes ONE inventory sample into move_group's persistent planning scene as a box CollisionObject. `object_id` is the sample id (`{{ item.sample_id }}`), `dimensions` is the inventory row's box dims spliced as the BARE whole-object placeholder (`{{ item.dimensions }}`), and `pose` is a geometry_msgs/PoseStamped whose inner `pose` is the inventory row's geometry_msgs/Pose spliced verbatim (`{{ item.pose }}`). Plain `executor` (NO per-step capacity): it inherits the enclosing cell lease's held `runner.<id>` namespace by containment, so every add_object hits the SAME held move_group scene. The embedded interface_typedefs shape the request live for this non-bundled service.

data.label: Reset Scene (clear move_group)
data.description: Cell-reset head — makes the whole run idempotent. ROS `call_service` to `/clear_scene` (`aithericon_motion_bridge/srv/ClearScene`, confirm-guarded) — removes ALL world + attached collision objects from move_group's persistent planning scene, so a re-run starts from a clean slate and any sample still attached to the gripper from a prior run is detached back into the world. Plain `executor` (NO per-step capacity): rides the enclosing cell lease's held `runner.<id>` namespace by containment, so the clear hits the SAME held move_group the seed + jobs plan against. Runs ONCE, before seeding.

data.label: Home Arm (reference config)
data.description: Cell-reset tail — brings the arm to a known reference joint configuration so every run's motion planning starts from the SAME deterministic state (and the arm visibly returns to neutral between runs). ROS `send_action_goal` to `/xarm6_traj_controller/follow_joint_trajectory` (`control_msgs/action/FollowJointTrajectory`, the shipped Path B execution primitive) with a single hand-authored waypoint to the xArm6 all-zeros home `[0,0,0,0,0,0]` (arm upright). This pose is deliberately COLLISION-FREE with the table-level rack samples seeded below (z≈0.28): a forward-leaning ready pose like `[0,-0.3,-0.3,0,0.6,0]` intersects a seeded sample box, so MoveIt rejects the pick plan with an in-collision START state (error 99999, planning_time 0) — verified live. Runs sequentially AFTER the scene clear and BEFORE seeding — nothing else drives the controller, so the goal reaches STATUS_SUCCEEDED with no preemption. Plain `executor`: rides the held lease by containment. The action RESULT rides `stdout_tail`, not outputs.

data.label: Load Jobs → array
data.description: Stages the bound `run_job` asset collection (alias `jobs`) as a token-resident JSON array + count so the sequential Loop can iterate it one record at a time — asset bindings are python-global stagings, not node outputs, so the Loop reads them via accumulators seeded from its `input` token (`input.items` / `input.count`, the `load_jobs` output flowing into the Loop's `in` port). Plain `executor`: rides the held cell lease by containment. Mirrors demo 41's `load_cells`.

data.label: Handle Jobs (sequential)
data.description: Iterates the staged `run_job` records ONE AT A TIME (sequential Loop, NOT a concurrent Map) so the single arm is never driven by two jobs at once — the previous Map fanned every job out in parallel, and two jobs racing the one `/xarm6_traj_controller` preempted each other (action STATUS_CANCELED). The job array is immutable, so the Loop needs NO accumulators: the body indexes the parked `load_jobs` output directly by the built-in counter (`load_jobs.items[job_loop.iteration]`, a body-Python parked-producer borrow), and the `loopCondition` borrows `load_jobs.count` (a guard borrow into the same parked producer — accumulator `init` is intentionally NOT borrow-scanned by the compiler, so a producer-seeded accumulator can't work; the direct borrow is the supported path). Each iteration's body resolves the current job's poses, a Decision dispatches on its `op` into the pick / place / swap SubWorkflows, then loops back. `loopCondition` runs the body exactly `count` times (the cap is checked AFTER body_out, so the bound is `iteration < count - 1`); `maxIterations` is a runaway backstop.

data.label: Resolve Job Poses
data.description: Loop body head. Python step: reads the CURRENT job from the Loop accumulators (`job_loop.items[job_loop.iteration]`) plus the bound `sample_rack` (alias `racks`) and `instrument_ports` (alias `ports`) asset collections, looks up the matching rack slot + instrument port, and emits the four poses as geometry_msgs/Pose JSON (`grasp_pose`, `grasp_approach`, `insert_pose`, `insert_approach`) so they splice straight into the child SubWorkflows' plan targets via the bare-placeholder whole-object path. Also echoes the current job's `op` (drives the Decision) and `sample_id` (wired into the SubWorkflows). Outputs are implicit (the runner sweeps globals matching the declared output port).

data.label: Dispatch on op
data.description: Compiler-checked gate on the current job's `op`, echoed by `resolve_poses`. `pick` → the 37-pick SubWorkflow; `place` → the 38-place SubWorkflow; anything else (`swap`) falls through to the default 39-swap SubWorkflow. Each guard resolves `resolve_poses.op` against that step's declared output port at compile time.

data.label: Pick (37)
data.description: Embeds the 37-pick child template. Plans to the resolved approach pose, gates the plan, executes (Path B), then grasps the sample. inputMapping wires the resolved grasp pose, approach pose, and the job's sample id into the child's Start.initial fields.

data.label: Place (38)
data.description: Embeds the 38-place child template. Plans to the resolved insert approach pose, gates, executes (Path B), then releases the sample at the instrument port. inputMapping wires the resolved insert pose, insert approach pose, and the job's sample id into the child's Start.initial fields.

data.label: Swap (39)
data.description: Default branch. Embeds the 39-swap child template (pick occupant from port → place to temp → pick new from rack → place to port). The eight swap inputs are mapped from the resolved poses and job fields as best the asset shapes allow: the port grasp/approach come from the resolved insert poses, and the rack grasp/approach come from the resolved grasp poses; the occupant/new ids both default to the job's sample id (the asset model carries one sample per job, so a real distinct occupant id would need a second asset lookup — recorded in notes for live verification). The temp poses reuse the rack poses.

data.label: Mark Job Done
data.description: Map body terminal. The Decision dispatches each job into exactly one of the pick/place/swap SubWorkflows, each of which declares its OWN result shape (`picked`/`placed`/`swapped`). The Map gathers a single `resultVar` (`done`) per element, so the branches must converge on one uniform terminal field. This trivial Python step emits `done: true`, giving the gather a present, typed `done` for every job regardless of which op ran.
